expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: scenario drafting distinguishes dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly covers (a) distinct dashboard-level export paths/entry points: “verify distinct entry points are available…” with entry point A vs B; (b) option combinations: “option combination A…” and “option combination B…” plus toggling options affecting button state; (c) visible completion outcomes: “visible ‘export completed / success’ outcome”, explicit error outcomes for auth denied and network/service failure, and “success outcome includes an actionable affordance (open/copy link)”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md states: “Produced a Phase 4a subcategory-only scenario draft…” and “Aligned to Phase 4a: subcategory-first structure, atomic nested steps, observable verification leaves, no canonical top-layer categories.” result.md matches this: subcategory headings (e.g., “Google Sheets export options…”, “completion outcomes…”) with nested atomic steps and observable outcomes, and includes a note: “intentionally avoids canonical top-layer grouping”.
